With same-day service available, we ensure Lawrence residents can enjoy their outdoor spaces without the threat of dangerous hornet stings.

We offer transparent pricing between $350-$550, comprehensive warranties, and understand the unique pest control needs of Lawrence's waterfront properties and historic homes.
localContext: Lawrence's tree-lined streets, waterfront locations along the bay, and mix of historic and modern homes create ideal nesting spots for hornets, particularly in eaves, attics, and mature trees. From the estates near Lawrence Country Club to the residential neighborhoods near Rock Hall Museum, our team knows the specific challenges hornet infestations present to Lawrence properties and responds quickly to protect your family and visitors.

faqs.question: How much does hornet removal cost in Lawrence?
faqs.answer: Our hornet removal services in Lawrence range from $350 to $550, depending on the size and location of the nest, accessibility, and severity of the infestation. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before beginning any work, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in Lawrence?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and emergency hornet removal services throughout Lawrence and the Five Towns area. Most hornet nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, and we can typically arrive at your Lawrence property within 2-4 hours of your call during business hours.

faqs.question: Are hornets dangerous to my family and pets in Lawrence?
faqs.answer: Yes, hornets can be very dangerous as they are aggressive when defending their nests and can sting multiple times. Unlike bees, hornets can attack in swarms and their stings are particularly painful and can cause severe allergic reactions. Professional removal is always recommended to protect your family and pets.

faqs.question: What's the difference between hornets, wasps, and yellow jackets?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than most wasps and yellow jackets, typically measuring 1-1.5 inches long, and build large paper nests usually in trees or under eaves. While all three are aggressive when threatened, hornets are generally more aggressive and their larger size means more venom per sting. Our Lawrence team can identify and safely remove all stinging insect nests.
